response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned full-stack engineer with 16+ years of broad experience, but his C++ exposure is extremely limited — roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 and a university thesis project. The role demands deep, sustained C++ and STL expertise as its primary requirement, alongside industrial-automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 secure development practices, multi-threaded systems design, and industrial protocol experience (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA). None of these appear substantively in his background. His career has followed a clear web and TypeScript trajectory, making him fundamentally misaligned with an on-premises industrial C++ platform role regardless of his general engineering quality.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship foundations including TDD, CI/CD, agile, and trunk-based development across multiple production environments.
Demonstrated event-driven and distributed-systems thinking through the GISCollective platform and high-throughput microservices work at Dunnhumby.
Node.js and React experience present, which the job description lists as advantageous secondary skills.
concerns: C++ experience amounts to roughly six months at National Instruments (2014–2015) plus a thesis project — nowhere near the 8–12 years of C++ depth the role requires.
No evidence of industrial-automation domain knowledge, IEC 62443 or SDL practices, or industrial communication protocols such as MQTT, Modbus TCP/IP, OPC UA, or IEC 61850.
Entire career trajectory is web and full-stack development (TypeScript, Node.js, React, MongoDB), which is structurally mismatched with an embedded/on-premises industrial platform context.
